text: Morning. I'm Vira — your AI chief of staff. This conversation is the tour.
Everything you see runs locally on this Mac. I read Messages, Contacts, Calendar, and mail in place; nothing leaves the machine except drafts you explicitly send to Claude.
What am I looking at right now?

Fixture mode — a demo dataset with one contact (me), so a fresh clone has something to show. The dossier on the left is what every real contact gets: relationship summary, conversation hooks, open loops, shared media.
My open loops are your setup checklist. Close them in order: config, Full Disk Access, your contact data, mail. Each one lights up a feature.

The Feed window is the live wire: every inbound iMessage and email, joined to its person, with unread state synced between desktop and phone. It starts working the moment Full Disk Access lands — no restart.
The Brief answers 'who and what deserves my attention today' — calendar, threads waiting on you, relationship loops going stale, contacts going quiet. Open it first thing in the morning.

Hybrid semantic search over every photo, link, and document ever shared with you — OCR, scene embeddings, faces, captions, transcripts, all indexed locally. Ask it questions in plain English; it corrects your memory when you misremember who sent what.
The Actions window is a Claude Code cockpit: every skill and command in your library gets a run button, and the Ideas backlog can dispatch coding agents at this very codebase — with a live terminal.
Shared media below this thread has my reading list for you. The suggested-reply buttons above the compose box work too — try drafting a reply to me.
When you're ready to make this real: data/config.json, then point crm_root at your people. I'll get out of the way.
